Sealed bid:
📘 How it works : In a Sealed Bid eAuction, participants submit confidential offers that remain hidden until the end. The most competitive bid wins.

Before the event: Participants submit bids before the deadline.

During the event: Bids remain undisclosed; participants may lower their offers until closure.

Timing: No live ranking; results revealed after the deadline.

Awarding: The most competitive bid secures the lot, based on awarding principles.
English:
📘 How it works : An English Reverse eAuction starts from suppliers’ pre-bids. Participants see their ranking and can submit improved offers until time expires. The final rank determines the winner.

Before the event: Participants submit a pre-bid that sets their initial rank.

During the event: Ranking updates dynamically with each new bid.

Timing: Runs for a limited time, with automatic extensions if bids are placed in the final minutes.

Awarding: Lots are awarded according to final ranking and defined awarding principles.
Dutch:
📘 How it works : A Dutch Reverse eAuction starts at a low price that increases step by step. The first participant to accept the price wins immediately.

Before the event: A pre-bid may be required.

During the event: Price rises in fixed increments each round until a bid is accepted.

Timing: Limited duration but ends instantly when a bid is placed.

Awarding: The first accepted bid secures the lot.
Dutch pref:
📘 How it works : A Preferred Dutch Reverse eAuction works like a Dutch auction, but selected suppliers receive priority access to each price step before others. The first accepted bid wins.

Before the event: Participants may submit pre-bids; preferred suppliers are designated.

During the event: Price rises in steps; preferred suppliers get first option at each step.

Timing: Limited duration but ends instantly when a bid is accepted.

Awarding: The first accepted bid secures the lot.
Jap:
📘 How it works : A Japanese Reverse eAuction starts at a high price that decreases in rounds. Participants must accept the new price each round to stay in the event. The last remaining bidder wins.

Before the event: A pre-bid may be required.

During the event: Price decreases in fixed steps; participants must stay active each round or exit the eAuction.

Timing: Limited duration, ending when only one participant remains or the minimum price is reached.

Awarding: The last active participant secures the lot.
Jap no award:
📘 How it works : A Japanese No Rank eAuction decreases prices in rounds, with participants required to stay active each round. Unlike a standard Japanese auction, awarding is decided outside the auction.

Before the event: A pre-bid may be required.

During the event: Price decreases in fixed steps; participants confirm each round or exit, with their exit price recorded.

Timing: Limited duration, ending once all rounds are complete.

Awarding: The final decision combines eAuction results with external criteria.

Sealed bid:
🎯 How to win?

Submit the most competitive bid, the supplier offering the best price for the agreed terms will win.
English:
🎯 How to win?  

In an English eAuction, the supplier who offers the lowest price by the end of the eAuction is awarded
Dutch:
🎯 How to win?  

 In a Dutch eAuction, the fastest wins : be the first to accept the price that works for you to secure the lot.
Jap:
🎯 How to win ?

In a Japanese eAuction, you must remain active by accepting the price at each round.
The last supplier to stay in the eAuction wins at the final accepted price.
Jap no award:
🎯 How to win ? 

In a Japanese No Rank eAuction, all suppliers must choose whether or not to accept the new price at each round to stay in the eAuction. 
The event continues through all rounds, regardless of how many participants remain active. At the end, no winner is awardeded, the buyer will attribute the lot outside the eAuction.

Sealed bid:
⏱ eAuction Timing Explained

The Sealed Bid eAuction has a fixed duration. During this time, you can update your bid as many times as you want.

When the timer runs out, all bids are automatically locked and the eAuction closes.
English:
⏱ eAuction Timing Explained

The English eAuction has a fixed duration, for example, 5 minutes. During this time, suppliers can place live bids to improve their rank.

To prevent last-second bidding, an additional period called Overtime is applied. If a bid is placed during this phase, the countdown automatically resets according to the chosen overtime setting (e.g., overtime 2mn).
Dutch:
⏱ eAuction Timing Explained

In a Dutch eAuction, the price increases gradually, usually every 15, 30 or 60 seconds. You’ll see a countdown timer and the current price updating in real time.

The eAuction ends immediately as soon as any supplier accepts a price.
Dutch pref:
⏱ eAuction Timing Explained

The price goes up in rounds (every 15, 30, or 60 seconds). Preferred suppliers get early access at each round (e.g., first 30 seconds). 
After that, all suppliers can bid. If no one bids, the eAuction moves to the next price step with the same priority.
Jap:
⏱ eAuction Timing Explained

The price drops step by step (every 15, 30, or 60 seconds). At each step, suppliers choose to stay or exit. Once you exit, your rank is shown. 
The auction ends when all suppliers have left.
Jap no award:
⏱ eAuction Timing Explained


The price drops step by step (every 15, 30, or 60 seconds). At each step, suppliers choose to stay or exit. 
The auction ends when all suppliers have left.

English:
⚙️ What is a pre-bid?

A pre-bid in an English eAuction is the initial offer you submit before the auction starts. It determines your starting rank when the eAuction begins
Dutch:
⚙️ What is a pre-bid?

A prebid is an offer you submit before the eAuction starts.

When the eAuction reaches your prebid level, the system will automatically place your bid. You'll win automatically the eAuction even if a competitor wants to place a bid at the same time. Pre-bid prior bids.

If two suppliers have placed the same pre-bid, the one who submitted it first will be awarded
Dutch pref:
⚙️ What is a pre-bid?

A prebid is an offer you submit before the eAuction starts.

When the eAuction reaches your prebid level, the system will automatically place your bid. You'll win automatically the eAuction even if a competitor wants to place a bid at the same time. Pre-bid prior bids.

   -If you’re a preferred supplier, your pre-bid will be triggered during your exclusive access window, as soon as your target price is reached.

   -If you’re not preferred, your pre-bid will be placed only once the round is open to all suppliers, and the price has reached your pre-set level.

If two suppliers have placed the same pre-bid, the one who submitted it first will be awarded
Jap:
⚙️ What is a pre-bid?

A pre-bid is an offer you submit before the event begins. The system will automatically accept all rounds until it reaches your pre-bid.

Once the eAuction reaches your pre-bid, you’ll need to manually confirm whether you want to stay in the eAuction.

If two suppliers have placed the same pre-bid, the one who submitted it first will be awarded
Jap no award:
⚙️ What is a pre-bid?

A pre-bid is an offer you submit before the event begins. The system will automatically accept all rounds until it reaches your pre-bid.

Once the eAuction reaches your pre-bid, you’ll need to manually confirm whether you want to stay in the eAuction.

Sealed bid:
🎰 What is a multi-lot ? :

A multi-lot eAuction means the eAuction is split into several distinct lots .

Each lot represents a specific item or products, and each lot is treated as an independent event :
Each lot has its own ranking.

English:
🔁 Transformation

A transformation means that bids are adjusted using buyer-defined factors such as quality, logistics, or delivery. 

These adjustments, called handicaps, can be either dynamic (applied once at the start) or fix (recalculated as bidding evolves). Suppliers only see the dynamic handicap.

Sealed bid:
💡 2 different scenarios that ends the eAuction

Win the eAuction : You're competing with other suppliers to offer the lowest price. You place a bid lower than all others.

Lose the eAuction : You place your bid, but another supplier submits a lower price.
English:
💡 2 different scenarios that ends the eAuction

Win the eAuction : You're competing with other suppliers to offer the lowest price. You place a bid lower than all others.

Lose the eAuction : You place your bid, but another supplier submits a lower price before time runs out.
Dutch:
💡 3 different scenarios that ends the eAuction

Win with pre-bid : You set a pre-bid before the eAuction starts. When the price reaches your pre-bid, the system places your bid automatically and you win. 

Lose : You placed a prebid, but another supplier made a bid before your price was reached.

Win by a live bid : You set a pre-bid, but before the price reached it, you placed a live bid by clicking “Place bid”, and won the eAuction.
Dutch pref:
💡 4 different scenarios that ends the eAuction

Win with pre-bid : You set a pre-bid before the eAuction. When the price reaches your level and access opens, the system places your bid automatically and you win. 

Win by Placing a Bid : You set a pre-bid before the eAuction starts, but before the price reaches it, you place a live bid by clicking “Place bid” when access opens, and won the eAuction.

Lose during restricted access : A preferred supplier bid during the restricted acces, you can't bid at the same time, you lose. 

Lose during open access : No ones bid during the restricted access but at the end of it, another supplier places a bid, you lose.
Jap:
💡 3 different scenarios that ends the eAuction

Win with pre-bid :Your pre-bid was the lowest among all suppliers. Since no one accepted a lower price, you win the eAuction with your pre-bid.

Lose : You’ve reached your pre-bid price. Since you don’t want to accept a lower price, you exit the eAuction here, while other suppliers continue.

Win with live bid : You’ve passed your pre-bid and kept accepting lower prices while others left. By staying in the eAuction longer, you win.
Jap no award:
💡 2 different scenarios for the eAuction


Pre-bid : You set a pre-bid. All prices are accepted until your pre-bid is reached, then you exit the eAuction

Live bid : You set a pre-bid, but when it’s reached you keep accepting new prices and stay in until you decide to leave.

Dutch:
💡 2 different scenarios that ends the eAuction

Win with a live bid : You placed a live bid by clicking “Place bid”, and won the eAuction.

Lose :  Another supplier made a live bid before you, you lose.
Dutch pref:
💡 3 different scenarios that ends the eAuction

Win by Placing a Bid : You placed a live bid by clicking “Place bid” when access opens, and won the eAuction.

Lose during restricted access : A preferred supplier bid during the restricted acces, you can't bid at the same time, you lose. 

Lose during open access : No ones bid during the restricted access but at the end of it, another supplier places a bid, you lose.
Jap:
💡 2 different scenarios that ends the eAuction

Win with live bid : You’ve accepted lower prices while others left. By staying in the eAuction longer, you win.

Lose : You’ve reached your lowest price. Since you don’t want to accept a lower price, you exit the eAuction here, while other suppliers continue.
